summary: A high‑energy late‑70’s NYC show captured on a decent audience tape – the music shines despite some crowd noise and occasional vocal roughness.
review: The performance is widely praised for its blistering Half‑Step, scorching Lazy Lightning → Supplication, soaring Scarlet > Fire and an unforgettable Terrapin.  Most listeners note that Keith Gatto’s Nak 300 audience tape is surprisingly clean, though the mix can be a bit noisy and Jerry’s voice sounds hoarse at times.  Overall the show is considered one of the standout late‑70s Dead concerts.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (≈75 %) are 4–5 stars and highlight exceptional musical moments, while the recording is described as good‑to‑very good for an audience source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Many reviewers call the tape “clean”, “real nice AUD” and appreciate Keith Gatto’s use of Nak 300 mics.  Complaints focus on crowd noise, occasional muffling and a marginal overall fidelity, but no catastrophic drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is praised for tight songwriting (Friend of the Devil, All Over Now, Brown Eyed Women) and a driving energy that carries into a second‑set jam marathon highlighted by the Lazy Lightning/Supplication sequence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar work described as “on fire” and “froggy”, but his vocal tone is often noted as hoarse or “shot” due to illness.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass playing gets a nod for solid support; Phil’s outro on All Over Now is specifically praised.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s vocals are called “madman‑like” and energetic; occasional off‑key moments are forgiven.
band_member_comments.Keys: Donna (keyboardist) is mentioned as being “on key” and adding to the overall power of the band.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum work is generally praised; Mickey’s antics (“falling off his chair”) are recalled fondly.
